t_ch: 你回来啦，怎么样，调查出什么了吗？
t_en: You're back. How did it go? Did you find anything?

t_ch: （告知自己的所见所想）
t_en: (Tell her your observation and your thoughts.)

t_ch: 听你这么说，三个人好像都有点问题，可是理论上来说，应该只有一名真正的感染者。
t_en: So, all three of them sound unusual. But in theory, there should only be one person who is infected.

t_ch: 可能三个人都感染了。
t_en: Maybe they are all infected.

t_ch: 不，这不符合「螺中笑声」上检测到的数据。
t_en: It can't be. It doesn't match the data detected in the Crackup Conch.

t_ch: 为什么你笃定只有一名？
t_en: Why are you so sure that only one person is infected?

t_ch: 因为我有科学验证过的检验数据。
t_en: Because I have test results that have been scientifically verified.

t_ch: 原理其实很简单，「螺中笑声」上遗留的模因病毒信息粒子的颗粒数量不高。
t_en: The principle behind it is actually quite simple. The viral load of informational particles left behind by the memetic virus in the Crackup Conch is simply not high enough.

t_ch: 满打满算勉强能达到一个成年人在感染后传播出来的极限值，所以绝不可能出现多人感染的情况。
t_en: The viral load detected barely reaches the amount that can be carried by an infected adult. So it's absolutely impossible for the Crackup Conch to have contracted the virus from multiple individuals.

t_ch: 但现在调查不出来…
t_en: But my investigation couldn't corroborate this...

t_ch: 没关系的，我早就设计好保底的方案了。
t_en: It's okay. I've got a backup plan.

t_ch: 那接下来怎么办？
t_en: So, what next?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，我可是留了后手的。
t_en: Hehe, I've got a backup plan.

t_ch: 模因病毒是通过虚拟数据传播的，而空间站里所有虚拟数据都会在终端机上留下完整的监控记录。
t_en: The memetic virus is transmitted through virtual data. And all the virtual data in the space station leave traces in the terminal.

t_ch: 既然之前调查不出什么来，那直接去查询这三位可疑人员的浏览记录，就能知道他们到底是哪位感染的了。
t_en: Since we couldn't get much from our previous investigation, let's check the browsing records of the three suspects. Then we should be able to identify the source of the infection.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，我之前为现在这种情况安排了紧急方案了。到这种时候，就要麻烦你跑一趟数据交换中心——也就是，终端机所在的机房。
t_en: Hehe, I've got an emergency plan for situations like this. Please visit the Data Exchange Center — where the terminal is located.

t_ch: 可我没用过终端机…
t_en: But I've never used a terminal...

t_ch: 安啦安啦，这个很简单的，进了机房通过身份验证，直接查询就好啦。
t_en: Don't worry. It's simple. Enter the terminal room, verify your identity, and you can start your query.

t_ch: 那你怎么不自己去？
t_en: Why don't you go yourself?

t_ch: 哎，我也是有苦衷的，这事说来话长。
t_en: *sigh* I have my own reasons. It's a long story.

t_ch: 因为终端机记录了每个数据设备使用者——也就是空间站上上下下所有人的使用记录，因此我也好，其他工作人员也好，都不方便去查。
t_en: Simply put, anyone using any data gadget, which basically includes everyone on the space station, would have their user behaviors recorded by the terminal. It is thus inappropriate for me or any other staff member to check such information.

t_ch: 呃，我还是不懂…
t_en: Um, I still don't get it.

t_ch: 别急，等我慢慢跟你解释。
t_en: Don't worry. I'll explain to you when I have time.

t_ch: 我知道，找借口偷懒呗。
t_en: I know. You are just looking for excuses to slack off.

t_ch: 才不是呢，要是我能去我早就去了。
t_en: It's not like that. I would have gone if I could.

t_ch: 你想想啊，空间站里往来人员的背景与身份各不相同，但在个人隐私方面大家都达成了一致。
t_en: Think about it. Despite different backgrounds and identities, people on the space station have reached an agreement on personal privacy.

t_ch: 终端机上能查到所有人使用虚拟数据留下的痕迹，比方说，你上班时间在访问一些奇怪的虚拟平台，在这儿都能看得到。
t_en: The traces left from using virtual data can be found on the terminal. For example, from here people can see all the unusual virtual platforms you visited during your working hours.

t_ch: 因此，基于尊重大家隐私的考量，我也好，其他工作人员也好，最好还是别碰终端机。
t_en: Therefore, to respect everyone's privacy, no one, including myself and the other staff, should access the terminal.

t_ch: 设身处地地想一下，假如你是空间站的工作人员，你能接受我去翻阅你在虚拟网络上的浏览记录这种事吗？
t_en: Think about it in their shoes, if you are space station staff, would you be happy with me going through your browsing records on the virtual network?

t_ch: 我宁可选择死亡…
t_en: I'd rather die...

t_ch: 正常人都接受不了吧。
t_en: It's unacceptable.

t_ch: 对啊，现在你该明白了吧？正因如此，你特殊的身份才是干这件事的最好人选！
t_en: Now you understand, right? That's why your special identity makes you the perfect candidate for this!

t_ch: 为啥？我不明白。
t_en: Why? I don't understand.

t_ch: 这就要从你的英雄事迹与特殊身份说起了。
t_en: Let me start with your heroic deeds and your special identity.

t_ch: 生怕我不会被打死？
t_en: So, you just want me to get beaten up?

t_ch: 诶，你成天都胡思乱想些什么呢？我和你无冤无仇的，怎么会害你。
t_en: Jeez, is this the kind of nonsense you think about all day? I have nothing against you. Why would I set you up?

t_ch: 从客观角度来看，你算是空间站的「外人」，跟大部分人都不熟，因此也不存在私交与针对某个人的可能。
t_en: From an objective point of view, you are an outsider on the space station. You are not familiar with most people here, so it's unlikely that you hold any personal grudge against anyone.

t_ch: 并非每个外来人都有资格去查阅大家的数据记录，但你不一样，你是拯救了空间站的英雄，大家都信得过你的人品。
t_en: Not every outsider has the right to access the data records. But you are different. You are the hero who saved the space station. People trust your moral character.

t_ch: 当然啦，如果你愿意帮忙，我希望你能对自己见到的所有数据记录都保密。
t_en: But of course, if you do decide to help, I hope you will keep everything you see strictly confidential.

t_ch: 没问题，我嘴巴很严的。
t_en: No problem. My lips are sealed.

t_ch: 哎，总之在空间站的时候全程保密就好，之后你要去哪儿做些什么我也管不着。
t_en: Anyway, keep it all a secret while you're still in the space station. As for where you go or what you do in the future, it's none of my business.

t_ch: 小事一桩，交给我了。
t_en: Piece of cake. Just leave it to me.

t_ch: 真的太感谢了！
t_en: Thank you so much!

t_ch: 在走之前，我先跟你讲讲终端机的使用规则吧。
t_en: Before you leave, let me tell you how to use the terminal.

t_ch: 因为之前空间站被入侵过，整个数据中心都遭受重创，终端机所在的机房也不例外。
t_en: As you know, the invasion of the station severely damaged the whole data center. And the server room where the terminal is housed is no exception.

t_ch: 尽管我们很努力地在维修了，但至今还没有完全恢复过来。之前发现了模因病毒感染者的事情后，我就只能派了个维修工程师每天去盯着。
t_en: Despite our best efforts, the damage is yet to be fully remediated. And reported cases of memetic virus infection only made things worse. I had to send a maintenance engineer to monitor the situation on a daily basis.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>工程师每天都会手动开启光学身份校验程序</color>，用来检验进入房间的每位访问者的生物电是否正常，以此确保终端机房间不被入侵。
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>The engineer would turn on the optical identity verification program manually every day</color> to check whether the bioelectricity of each visitor entering the room is normal, thus ensuring that the terminal room is not invaded.

t_ch: 由于光学身份校验程序是<color=#dbc291ff>在每天开启后动态刷新的，因此你每次进去都需要重新校验身份。</color>
t_en: Since the optical identity verification program is <color=#dbc291ff>refreshed dynamically after it is turned on every day, you need to re-verify your identity every time you enter.</color>

t_ch: 至于更多的细节，就<color=#dbc291ff>请你找维修工程师咨询</color>吧，进入房间的权限与相关事项我已经和工程师交代过了。
t_en: For more details, please <color=#dbc291ff>consult the maintenance engineer</color>. I've told the engineer that you have permission to enter the room.

t_ch: 那么，接下来就拜托你了！
t_en: So... I will leave the rest to you!

t_ch: 终端机屏幕上跳出一行大字：<color=#eb4d3dff>「错误代码<unbreak>505</unbreak>：服务不可用」</color>
t_en: A row of large words pops up on the terminal screen: <color=#eb4d3dff>"Code error <unbreak>505</unbreak>: Service unavailable."</color>

t_ch: 除此以外便没有任何回应。
t_en: There are no other responses.

t_ch: 有人喘着气急急忙忙跑着回到了房间，看起来，他就是艾丝妲所说的<color=#dbc291ff>「维修工程师」</color>。
t_en: Someone runs back into the room, panting. Looks like he's the <color=#dbc291ff>"maintenance engineer"</color> Asta mentioned.

t_ch: 既然终端机没有反应，不如去问问他好了。
t_en: The terminal doesn't respond. Let me ask him.

t_ch: 抱——呼呼——抱歉、抱歉！
t_en: Sorry — huff puff — I'm sorry!

t_ch: 你就是{NICKNAME}吧？艾丝妲站长说要来用终端机的那位——她之前已经跟我交代好了。
t_en: Are you {NICKNAME}? Asta already gave me a heads up that you are going to come use the terminal.

t_ch: 非常抱歉，刚刚隔壁机组的系统突然自动更新了，有几个科室的研究员还没来得及保存成果，我得过去帮忙。
t_en: I'm terribly sorry. The computer system in the next room suddenly initiated an auto-update. The researchers didn't have time to save their results. So I went to help them just now.

t_ch: 终端机没反应了。
t_en: The terminal did not respond.

t_ch: 提示我错误代码<unbreak>505</unbreak>？
t_en: Prompted error code <unbreak>505</unbreak>?

t_ch: 啊，是因为我刚刚不在，就没把终端机的数据联通。等我先开一下，很快就好——
t_en: Ahh, I didn't connect the data to the terminal while I was away. Let me turn it on. It'll just take a second...

t_ch: 现在好了！麻烦您再过一次身份校验程序，然后直接查询终端机就会有结果了。
t_en: It's all set now! Please verify your identity again. And the results will come up when you input your query.

t_ch: （原来梅厄之前一直失眠，但在看了猫科萌宠打呼噜的短视频后就能睡得着了。）
t_en: (It turns out Mare had been suffering from insomnia. Now she can fall asleep after watching short videos of cats snoring.)

t_ch: （之前她嘴里神神叨叨的念词，也只是在模仿猫科动物的叫声。）
t_en: (So she was just imitating the sound of cats last time.)

t_ch: （原来她只是个普通的云养猫爱好者啊，那没事了，感染者不是她。）
t_en: (Turns out she's just a run-of-the-mill cat lover. That's all good. She can't be the one who got infected.)

t_ch: （再查查其他人的记录好了……）
t_en: (Let me check the other people's records...)

t_ch: 终端机屏幕上跳动出一行大字：<color=#eb4d3dff>「当前算力不足，请稍后再试。」</color>
t_en: A row of large words pops up on the terminal screen: <color=#eb4d3dff>"Insufficient computing power. Please try again later."</color>

t_ch: 但连试了很多次依然还是这行提示，或许终端机的时间概念并非人类世界里的「稍后」。
t_en: After several attempts, the same message still keeps popping up. Perhaps the terminal meant "a lot later."

t_ch: 还是找<color=#dbc291ff>维修工程师</color>来修一下吧。
t_en: I'd better ask the <color=#dbc291ff>maintenance engineer</color> to fix it.

t_ch: 怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong?

t_ch: 只查到了一份记录。
t_en: I only found one record.

t_ch: 能查到就行…噢，你是在问其他的啊。
t_en: Good... Oh, you mean you couldn't find the others?

t_ch: 提示「算力不足，稍后再试」？
t_en: "Insufficient computing power. Try again later?"

t_ch: 噢噢，这个正常，终端机没坏，吓我一跳。
t_en: Oh, that's normal. The terminal is not broken. It's nothing to worry about.

t_ch: 是这样的，那个提示说「稍后再试」，实际上「稍后」没用的，要等到第二天才能用。
t_en: Although, it won't be operational until tomorrow.

t_ch: 这是为什么呢？
t_en: Why?

t_ch: 因为之前空间站被入侵过，本来终端机不止一台，联通的也远非现有规模的机组。
t_en: Because the station was invaded. Originally, we had more than one terminal, connecting units on a much larger scale.

t_ch: 但在之前的动荡中，有些机组被毁坏了，终端机的算力就大为下降，更别说现在还是单核运转……
t_en: During that debacle, some units were damaged. The computing power of the terminal was thus greatly decreased, not to mention it's only running on a single-core processor right now...

t_ch: 站长说已经在准备采购新的设备了，不过眼下还没到货。所以，以目前的算力来说，您每天只能查询到一次结果。
t_en: The lead researcher said she was going to purchase new equipment. But it hasn't arrived yet. Therefore, with the current computing power, you can only query once a day.

t_ch: 而且现在的身份校验程序，也只能由我每天手动开启，所以…还是麻烦您<color=#dbc291ff>明天再来</color>一趟吧，记得来找我开校验。
t_en: And the current identity verification program has to be activated manually by me every day, so... please <color=#dbc291ff>come back tomorrow</color>. Don't forget to ask me to turn on the verification.

t_ch: 你来啦！
t_en: Here you are!

t_ch: 帮我开下终端机。
t_en: Please turn on the terminal for me.

t_ch: 好嘞，走你！光学身份校验程序已经架设完毕，终端机目前算力充足，您直接用就完事了。
t_en: Yup, here you go! The optical identity verification program has been set up. The terminal has sufficient computing power. Please feel free to use it.

t_ch: （虚拟网络放大了人心中的恶意，看他被骂成这样，难道是在最脆弱的时候被模因病毒趁虚而入？）
t_en: (People act much more maliciously on the virtual network. He was brutally harassed online. Did he get infected by the memetic virus during his most vulnerable moments?)

t_ch: （可他说话的内容与发帖的文风，都与艾丝妲说的「毒鸡汤」有着显著不同，恐怕感染者并不是他。）
t_en: (But his speech and the style of his posts are significantly different from the "cliché quotes" Asta had mentioned. I'm afraid he's not the infected one.)

t_ch: （那就是乔安了？不过要下定论的话，还是得查一下乔安的记录才行。）
t_en: (Then is it Joanne? But I better not jump to a conclusion just yet. I should check Joanne's records.)

t_ch: 终端机屏幕上跳动出一行大字：<color=#eb4d3dff>「查询失败，点击重新加载。」</color>
t_en: A row of large words pops up on the terminal screen: <color=#eb4d3dff>"Query failed. Click to refresh."</color>

t_ch: 然而点击了也依然是这行冰冷的提示，还是去问问维修工程师吧。
t_en: However, no matter how many times you click, the same message still pops up. I better ask the maintenance engineer.

t_ch: 呃，又坏了？
t_en: What? Is it broken again?

t_ch: 一直要我重新加载…
t_en: It kept asking me to refresh...

t_ch: 看来还是兼容性出了问题……
t_en: It seems to be some compatibility issues...

t_ch: 「又坏了」？
t_en: Broken "again?"

t_ch: 呃，维修期间，偶尔坏一下也挺常见的。
t_en: Well, it sometimes breaks down during maintenance.

t_ch: 哎，可惜了，我今天才找到了之前的几台表面看上去完好的旧机组，组装了进去想提升下终端机算力的。
t_en: Oh no! This is caused by the old units I installed into the terminal today. They looked good to me at the time and I thought they could boost the terminal's computing power.

t_ch: 看来还是得等新机组到货才能彻底解决这个问题。
t_en: Looks like we'll have to wait until the replacement units are delivered.

t_ch: 抱歉啊，您别着急，我等会儿就去把这几台机组拆下来。保证不会影响到现有算力。
t_en: Sorry, please give me some time. Let me remove these units. I assure you the existing computing power won't be affected.

t_ch: 不过呢，还是得麻烦您<color=#dbc291ff>明天再来</color>找我一趟了，到明天肯定是有算力的，这一点您放心！
t_en: However, I'm afraid you'll have to <color=#dbc291ff>come back tomorrow</color>. I'm sure by tomorrow there will have been sufficient computing power!

t_ch: 今天的算力是够的，我和站长汇报过您的事，她说预估您今天就能彻底完成此事了。
t_en: We've got enough computing power today. I've reported it to the lead researcher. She said she expects you to get this done today.

t_ch: 劳驾您确认完成了之后，过来知会我一声，我得把终端机的安保系统重新加固一下。
t_en: Please let me know when you are done. I'll need to reinforce the terminal's security system.

t_ch: 行，帮我开下终端机。
t_en: Sure. Please turn on the terminal for me.

t_ch: 光学身份校验程序已经架设完毕，终端机目前算力充足，请便！
t_en: The optical identity verification program is all set. The terminal has sufficient computing power. Please help yourself!

t_ch: （明明字单独看我还认识，摆一起就看不懂了，而且看完感觉发晕，好想吐……）
t_en: (I understand every single word but I don't understand when they are put together. I feel dizzy reading them. I want to throw up...)

t_ch: （…究竟，我存在于这个世界上的意义是什么呢？人如果没有自己的事业，和太空垃圾有什么区别呢……）
t_en: (...What on earth justifies my continued existence in this world? If a person is without any career, would they be any different from a piece of space garbage...)

t_ch: （等等，我在胡思乱想些什么——好险，差点就被模因病毒感染了！）
t_en: (Hold on. What was I thinking? That was close! I almost got infected by the memetic virus!)

t_ch: （从乔安的记录来看，这个模因病毒会干扰人的心智，蛊惑人进行无意义地内卷，这种病毒真是害人不浅！）
t_en: (From Joanne's record, it is clear that this memetic virus interferes with one's mind and manipulates people into a pointless rat race. This virus is truly vicious!)

t_ch: （接下来该回去了，回去之前，还是跟工程师说一下吧，毕竟答应过他……）
t_en: (I should head back. But before I go, I better tell the engineer. After all, I promised him...)

t_ch: 您弄好了？
t_en: Are you done?

t_ch: 对，全部都查出来了。
t_en: Yes, I think I got the result.

t_ch: 好的，按照空间站数据中心的流程，您的查询权限会在您离开机房后关闭，感谢您的配合。
t_en: Okay. According to the space station data center's regulations, your query access will be terminated as soon as you leave the terminal room. Thank you for your cooperation.

t_ch: 我看完所有数据记录了。
t_en: I've gone through all the data records.

t_ch: 太好了，我听维修工程师之前向我汇报，因为先前入侵遗留的复兴工作还未全部完成，终端机的算力一直拖累你的进度。
t_en: Great. I heard from the maintenance engineer that, due to incomplete recovery from the previous invasion, the terminal's computing power had been hindering your progress.

t_ch: 没想到你这么快就查出来了，真是辛苦了，所以结论是？
t_en: So I didn't expect you to finish the investigation so soon. Thank you for your hard work. So what's the conclusion?

t_ch: （告知自己的所见与分析）
t_en: (Tell her your observation and your analysis.)

t_ch: 哈哈，原来如此，我之前就怀疑乔安是感染者，只不过先前一直没有找到实质性的证据。
t_en: Haha, I see. I did have a suspicion that Joanne was the one who got infected. But I didn't have any substantial evidence.

t_ch: 你怎么知道是她？
t_en: How did you know it was her?

t_ch: 很简单，三个人中只有她的表现出来的风格，是和「螺中笑声」后来讲的那些话是风格一致的。
t_en: Simple. Out of the three of them, she is the only one who exhibited speech patterns similar to those of the Crackup Conch after its infection.

t_ch: 我就说她成天打瞌睡的人怎么会突然「洗心革面」，这样突然抹掉自己的习惯与喜好，实在是太可疑了。
t_en: I've always found it rather suspicious that she, a person who dozes off all the time, would have such a drastic change of personality and would quit all her habits and hobbies out of nowhere.

t_ch: 你就不怀疑另外两个？
t_en: Have you ever suspected the other two?

t_ch: 他们俩的表现在你看来或许有些奇怪，但在我眼里却是再稀疏平常不过了。
t_en: Their behaviors may seem strange to you, but to me, they are extremely normal.

t_ch: 先说梅厄，上班走神是每个人类在工作中的必经之路，就算是我在从事自己挚爱领域的研究时，这种事也偶尔会发生。
t_en: Let's start with Mare. Getting distracted at work is such a common occurrence for all humans. It sometimes happens to me, even when I'm working in my favorite field of research.

t_ch: 再说克里斯，相比起其他科员，他才来空间站没多久，此前听说是个学业优异的贫苦学子。在来到遥远太空后肯定会有不适应的情况出现。
t_en: Then there's Chris. He is said to be a good student from a poor family. And compared with the other researchers, he's still fairly new here on the space station. So it's normal that he would experience discomfort after a lengthy space flight.

t_ch: 唯一令我意外的是，他的不适应程度有点严重。但我已经安排了医师去给他诊断一下了。
t_en: What surprised me was the severity of his discomfort. So I've already sent for a doctor to check on him.

t_ch: 结果还是不出我所料，接下来得清除一下乔安感染的病毒，让她「重回正轨」了。
t_en: Anyway, the overall result is as expected. Next, we should weed out Joanne's virus and get her "back on track" to her old self.

t_ch: 辛苦你忙前忙后的这么久，查到了实质性证据，那么——
t_en: Thank you for your hard work on finding such substantial evidence, so—

t_ch: 别急，我有几个问题想问。
t_en: Hold on, I have a few questions.

t_ch: 嗯？你尽管问，我知无不答。
t_en: Oh? Go ahead. I'll answer all your questions if I know the answers.

t_ch: 我还是有点没搞懂…
t_en: I still don't get it...

t_ch: 诶？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: 所以你是还有哪里需要我再讲解一下吗？
t_en: Is there anything else I need to clarify?

t_ch: 让乔安继续这样不好吗？
t_en: Isn't it a good thing to keep Joanne this way?

t_ch: …你这样的想法很危险啊，这可不行，乔安是个大活人，你怎么能这么想。
t_en: ...That was a dangerous thought. Of course not. Joanne is a living human being. You shouldn't think like that.

t_ch: 虽说大家有着不同的文化背景，但有些最基础的权益却是大家公认的底线，万万不可剥夺。
t_en: Although we come from different cultural backgrounds, some of the most basic rights and interests are recognized by all of us as the common bottom line and must not be deprived.

t_ch: 再说了，根据我的经验——主要从我自己的人生来看，不是发自内心喜爱自己研究的领域，再怎么苦干也不会有什么出彩结果的。
t_en: Besides, based on my personal experience, you'll never get great results in any field of research unless you are passionate about it.

t_ch: 你是怎么猜到乔安有问题的？
t_en: How did you guess correctly that Joanne was the one?

t_ch: 猜？这并不算「猜」，从某些意义来说，算得上是一种简单易懂的心理科学吧。
t_en: Guess? I didn't "guess." To some extent, it was just simple psychology.

t_ch: 因为乔安之前完全抹掉了自己的个人生活，这非常不合理。用个简单的比喻来说，人的承受力是有限的，就像气球那样——
t_en: Joanne completely wiped out her personal life, which is very unusual. Think about this simple analogy. People's capacity for work is limited, just like how a balloon can only hold a finite amount of air...

t_ch: 在缺乏兴趣爱好的满足与休息的情况下，人就像一只被源源不断注入气体的气球，不放气的话，只有崩溃这个必然的结局。
t_en: When they don't get enough rest or fulfillment, people will just be like a balloon being unceasingly inflated, which will inevitably blow up if there's no chance for an air release.

t_ch: 你不担心另外两位的行为吗？
t_en: Aren't you worried about the other two?

t_ch: 担心什么？你是指会影响到他们在空间站的研究工作吗？除了克里斯需要医师进行心理疏导之外，我不觉得有什么问题。
t_en: Worried about what? Do you mean how their research at the space station will be affected? I don't think there's any problem except that Chris might need some psychological counseling from a doctor.

t_ch: 每个人都有自己的人生，在不影响到空间站安危与违背其他规定的情况下，做什么都是大家的自由。
t_en: People have the freedom to do whatever they want in their own lives, as long as they don't undermine the security of the space station or violate the regulations.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 唔，我大概明白你心中的疑惑了。从短视点的目光看来，无视大家的安危，让他们无止尽地工作，看起来是好事，实则祸害无穷。
t_en: Well, I do get your point to some extent. It might seem productive, at least in the short term, to just disregard people's safety concerns and force them into nonstop work. But in the real world, such practices will only spell disaster.

t_ch: 空间站建设在宇宙之中，离地面很远，并非每个人都能适应这里的生活。这一点在待久了以后会尤为明显。
t_en: The station is built in space, far away from any land surface. Not everyone can adapt to life here. This will become clear after a while.

t_ch: 虽说这里的设备与资源都是顶级的，但待久了依然有人会觉得憋屈难受。
t_en: Despite our top-notch equipment and resources, some people still find it unbearable to live on the station after staying here for a long time.

t_ch: 在这种情况下，适当地让大家发展自己的兴趣爱好，反而能保障大家离地太久后的心理健康，这才是提高大家效率的最佳方法。
t_en: In that case, letting people develop their own hobbies improves everyone's mental health, especially when they have been away from land for an extended period of time. This is the best way to improve overall productivity.

t_ch: 毕竟我们生来是人，并非什么没有情感的草木山石，如果连自己的喜好与情感都要抛之脑后，那我们又怎能称之为人？
t_en: After all, we are born to be humans, not plants or rocks that do not have emotions. How could we still call ourselves humans if we were to throw away our hobbies and emotions?

t_ch: 但我好像没什么爱好…
t_en: But I don't seem to have any hobbies...

t_ch: 你的事情我也知道一些，没关系的，你还有无限的未来，在漫长的人生里总能找到自己的喜好与要走的路。
t_en: Well, I do know a little about your situation. It's alright. You still have a long way to go. You'll eventually find your hobbies and your own way of life.

t_ch: 说得挺有道理的。
t_en: That makes sense.

t_ch: 那可不嘛，这也是我自己的人生经验，研究自己喜欢的东西，这样的人生才有滋有味。
t_en: Of course it does. This is also my life experience. Having fun in life is all about investing in things that you find interesting.

t_ch: 好啦，以后的路长着呢，你在外探索的时候，记好啦——无论发生什么事，都不要丢掉自己的喜好与情感。
t_en: Well, you still have a long way ahead of you. When you are out there exploring, remember — no matter what, don't just throw away your hobbies and your emotions.

t_ch: 啊对了，跟你说了这么多，差点忘记一件正事。
t_en: Oh right, by the way, I almost forgot one thing.

t_ch: 我知道终端机那边会打印出纸质记录，那几张纸页你自己收好，不要在空间站中外泄出去，这事你要答应我，否则报酬我可要收回去啦。
t_en: I know paper records will be printed out at the terminal. Keep those records to yourself. Don't let the information leak out in the space station. Promise me, or I'll take back the reward.

t_ch: 放心吧，绝不外泄。
t_en: Don't worry, they will never leak out.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，那么——总之这次就辛苦你啦，给你准备了点报酬，不是什么特别贵重的东西，不过都是我亲手准备的。
t_en: Well, anyway, thank you so much. This is the reward for you. I made this myself.

t_ch: 咳咳，你要是都不喜欢，回头我看看有新一代旗舰产品了，再带你去选购下歼星舰吧，现在年轻人之间都流行这个。
t_en: Ehem, if you don't like it, I can take you to buy the starnought fleet when the latest version comes out. It's very popular among young people these days.

t_ch: 军团虽已退走，异变仍在发生。空间站多处区域出现了裂界化的征兆，为此在艾丝妲的远程支援下，你深入现场展开了对未知危机的调查。
t_en: The Legion has retreated, but the mutation hasn't stopped. There are signs of Fragmentum in multiple areas. With remote support from Asta, you go deep into the field to investigate the unknown crisis.

t_ch: 看来你已经顺利抵达了现场，那我们长话短说。
t_en: Looks like you've already reached the site. So let me cut to the chase.

t_ch: 根据监控到的影像，军团残党和裂界造物同时存在于你现在所处的这片区域。
t_en: According to the surveillance video, creations of the Fragmentum and remnants of the Legion are present in this area where you are now.

t_ch: 我们的目的是调查这里裂界化加速的原因，为此需要你尽可能地对全域进行探索。
t_en: Our aim is to investigate the cause of the accelerated appearance of Fragmentum in this area, so you need to explore the entire area as much as possible.

t_ch: 你的相机上搭载了一道临时识别程序，通过它我能远程为你分析镜头内敌方的情报。
t_en: Your camera is equipped with a scanning program through which I can remotely analyze any visible enemy's intelligence for you.

t_ch: 前面好像有怪物出没，正好可以用来测试一下。
t_en: There appear to be some monsters right ahead, so let's give it a test run.

t_ch: 是反物质军团的残党，似乎只是落单的普通虚卒，没有什么特别之处。
t_en: It was a remnant of the Antimatter Legion. Seems like it was just an ordinary voidranger that had fallen off the grid, nothing special.

t_ch: 是裂界造物，它们诞生于裂界，身上还有诸多谜团未曾解开。
t_en: Those are Fragmentum creations, creatures born of the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 看来疏散人员的时候，两边的闸门都被锁死了。
t_en: It seems that the gates on both sides were locked during the evacuation.

t_ch: 受裂界影响，我没有办法远程解除封锁。想要通过，得先找到通行密钥。
t_en: Due to interference from the Fragmentum, I can't unlock them. You'll have to find the encryption key in order to get through.

t_ch: 等等，通行密钥上的涂层是特殊的光敏材料，相机上的识别程序或许能够派上用场。
t_en: Wait, the coating on the encryption key is made of a special photosensitive material. The camera's scanning function might be of some use here.

t_ch: 前面有多个敌方反应。假设密钥落在它们手上的话，或许可以找出并针对性地打倒密钥持有者以回避多余的战斗风险。
t_en: There are multiple enemies ahead. Assuming the key is in their hands, it may be possible to identify and target the key holder in order to avoid fighting more enemies than necessary.

t_ch: 这里似乎没什么东西了。
t_en: There doesn't seem to be much here.

t_ch: 去前面看看吧，那里的敌方反应十分密集，说不定能找到剩余的密钥。
t_en: Let's go check up ahead. Enemies reaction is very dense, and you may be able to find the remaining keys.

t_ch: 这是岔路口右侧房间的通行密钥，看来得麻烦你原路返回一趟了。
t_en: This is the encryption key for the room on the right side of the path, so it looks like you'll have to make a trip back the way you came.

t_ch: 只剩中央房间的大门没打开了，这应该就是那里的密钥。
t_en: The only door still locked is the one in the central room. This should be the key.

t_ch: 小心点，在这个家伙身上侦测到了不同寻常的能量波动。
t_en: Be careful, unusual energy fluctuations were detected in this guy.

t_ch: 看来这片区域裂界化加速的现象很难说跟它没有关系，去看一看吧。
t_en: It seems that the accelerated Fragmentum corrosion in this area can hardly be unrelated to it. Let's take a look.

t_ch: 干掉了么？能量波动趋于平稳…这片区域「裂界化」加速的趋势也得到了有效遏止。
t_en: Did you take care of it? The energy fluctuations have leveled off... and the accelerated Fragmentum corrosion has effectively halted in this area.

t_ch: 看来的确如我所料，辛苦你了{NICKNAME}。
t_en: It's as I expected. Good work, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 任务完成！后续的调查就交给我们吧。
t_en: Mission complete! We'll take care of the follow-up investigation.

t_ch: 黑塔•全景系统提示您：已锁定监控目标，无特殊光学反应。
t_en: Prompt from the Herta Panopticon System: Locked on to target, no special optical response.

t_ch: 黑塔•全景系统提示您：已锁定监控目标，通行密钥已识别。
t_en: Prompt from the Herta Panopticon System: Locked onto target, identifying key.

t_ch: 使用相机识别程序，找出身上藏有房间钥匙的敌人。
t_en: Using the camera's scanning function, find the enemy with the key hidden on their body.

t_ch: 看来你已经顺利抵达了现场，那我们长话短说，你现在所处的这片区域已被裂界造物侵占。
t_en: Now you've reached the site, let me cut to the chase. Fragmentum creatures are present in this area.

t_ch: 我们先前架设的设备也已损坏，此行的目的是收集信标数据，以便恢复远程连接。
t_en: The equipment we set up previously has been damaged. Therefore, this trip is to collect beacon data to restore the remote connection.

t_ch: 我在你的相机上搭载了一道临时识别程序，空间站的终端能远程为你解析镜头内的信标数据。
t_en: Your camera is equipped with a temporary scanning program, through which the space station's terminals can remotely analyze the data in any visible beacons.

t_ch: 你成功地从呜呜伯处提取到了信标数据，呜呜伯向你发出呜声致谢，便转身离去。
t_en: You succeed in extracting the beacon data from the Wubbaboo. It thanks you with a "woo" then leaves.

t_ch: 当前敌人已击败，信标数据已提取。
t_en: The current enemy has been defeated. Beacon data extracted.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你来了？艾丝妲站长吩咐我们在此处等你。这片区域的设备损毁状况十分严重，我们得留在原地架设终端。收集信标数据的事就拜托你了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, is that you? Lead Researcher Asta ordered us to wait for you here. The damage to the equipment in this place is so severe that we have to stay put and rig a new terminal. I'll leave it to you to gather the beacon data.

t_ch: 这些信标数据应该够了，带回去交给调查人员吧。
t_en: That should be enough data from the beacons. Take it back to the investigators.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，多亏有你在，远程连接已经连通。接下来的事情就交给我们了，请回去好好休息吧。
t_en: We're so lucky to have you, {NICKNAME}. The remote connection is up. Leave the rest to us. Go back and have a good rest.

t_ch: （提交信标数据）
t_en: (Submit beacon data)

t_ch: 信标数据收集完毕，正在建立连接…远程连接已成功！谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。接下来的事就交给我们了。请好好休息吧。
t_en: The beacon data's collected. Establishing connection... Remote connection successful! Thanks, {NICKNAME}, leave the rest to us. You go rest up.

t_ch: 一楼区域的设备也遭了殃，看来还得麻烦你再跑一趟了。
t_en: The equipment on the first floor has been hit, too. Looks like I'll have to ask you to take another trip.

t_ch: 临时识别程序运转正常，请多留意相机提示，接下来它或许会派上用场。
t_en: The temporary scanning program is working normally. Please pay attention to the camera hints, as they may come in handy later.

t_ch: 前方有个神秘的「大家伙」，收集信标数据的时候请多加小心。
t_en: There's a mysterious "big guy" up ahead. Please be careful while gathering data.

t_ch: 未能检测到有效信标
t_en: No valid beacon detected

t_ch: 检测到有效信标，正在连接
t_en: Valid beacon detected. Connecting...

t_ch: 前方侦测到多个敌方反应，有不少信标数据落在了它们手里，识别程序或许能够派上用场。
t_en: Multiple enemies detected ahead. They possess a large amount of beacon data. The scanner program might come in handy here.

t_ch: 这个舱段有不少呜呜伯出没，它们在混乱中将部分信标数据转移到了身上。
t_en: There're quite a lot of Wubbaboos in this zone, and they've managed to transfer some of the beacon data onto themselves amidst the chaos.

t_ch: 将镜头对准呜呜伯，按钮发光的时候就提取信标数据。
t_en: Point the camera at the Wubbaboo and extract data once the button lights up.

t_ch: 这个舱段的裂界化程度非常严重，最近就连终端设备也坏掉了，只能重建远程连接，但这需要信标数据……
t_en: The extent of Fragmentum corrosion in this zone is very severe, and even the terminal devices recently broke. Establishing a remote connection is the only way to go, but that requires beacon data...

t_ch: 你是{NICKNAME}？是艾丝妲站长派你来的吧，太好了，请你帮个忙，帮我们收集里面的信标数据吧，拜托了！
t_en: Are you {NICKNAME}? I guess you're sent here by Lead Researcher Asta. Awesome! Please do us a favor and collect data from the beacons inside. Please!

t_ch: 里面还是挺危险的…不过你要是准备好了，就和我说吧。
t_en: It's quite dangerous in there... But if you're ready, just let me know.

t_ch: 现在出发吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 还是再等等吧。
t_en: Maybe later.

t_ch: 你可算来了。事态紧急，我就直奔主题啦——阿兰他失踪了。
t_en: Finally, you're here. This is urgent, so I'll cut to the chase — Arlan's disappeared.

t_ch: 失踪了？
t_en: Disappeared?

t_ch: 没错，人找不到，消息不回，通讯也不接。
t_en: Yes, I can't find him. He's not answering his messages or calls.

t_ch: 我把他捡回来到现在，从来没发生过这种事。
t_en: In all the time since I took him in, this has never happened.

t_ch: 有查过监控录像吗？
t_en: Have you checked the surveillance cameras?

t_ch: 当然，但监控录像中也找不到阿兰的行踪。
t_en: Of course! I didn't see him...

t_ch: 「军团」入侵时破坏的监控系统还没完全恢复，可能是监控的盲区被利用了。
t_en: Parts of the surveillance system were destroyed when the Legion invaded — it still hasn't been fully repaired. Maybe he took advantage of a blind spot.

t_ch: 距他上次和我联系已经超过了<unbreak>13</unbreak>个小时，平时回复消息都不会超过<unbreak>13</unbreak>秒的！
t_en: It's been thirteen hours since he last contacted me. He usually takes less than <unbreak>13</unbreak> seconds to reply to my messages!

t_ch: 我怎么都找不到他，只好调取了防卫科的个人工作规划记录，然后发现了这个！
t_en: I looked everywhere and couldn't find him, so I checked the Security Department's work plan records. This is what I found!

t_ch: 哎，你看最后的工作目标：从心、从镜、从我。这怎么看都很怪吧？
t_en: Look, at his last work objectives entry: "Of the heart. Of the mirror. Of the self." It's so strange...

t_ch: 上周的记录也蛮怪的。
t_en: The entries for last week are pretty odd too.

t_ch: …啊哈哈，不、不要在意细节。
t_en: ...Ahaha, d—don't worry about those.

t_ch: 确实有点蹊跷。
t_en: Sure is.

t_ch: 果然{NICKNAME}也这么想吧？
t_en: I knew it. {NICKNAME}, you think so too, right?

t_ch: 是他这个年纪会写的。
t_en: I think guys his age do tend to write like this.

t_ch: 阿兰他没那么文艺啦！
t_en: ...B—But he's never written poetry before!

t_ch: 我问了防卫科，可他们都觉得这不是什么要紧事。还说什么：
t_en: *sigh* I asked people in the Security Department, but none of them thought it was concerning. They said stuff like:

t_ch: 「阿兰大哥已经是个成熟的男人了，有自己的安排也很正常！」
t_en: "Brother Arlan is a grown man with his own plans!"

t_ch: 我跟他们完全说不通，所以这件事只能拜托你了。
t_en: I couldn't get them to listen at all, so I'm asking you for help.

t_ch: 你都没有线索，我能怎办…
t_en: You don't have any clues, how can I—

t_ch: 还有一个秘密武器！那就是……
t_en: I have a secret weapon! Say hello to...

t_ch: 成熟的男人失踪了也正常！
t_en: Guys his age just need "alone time" sometimes!

t_ch: 你怎么也来防卫科那一套！不要开玩笑了，快帮帮我呀。
t_en: Why are you acting like those Security Department idiots!? Stop fooling around and help me!

t_ch: 我还有一个秘密武器！那就是……
t_en: I have a secret weapon! Say hello to...

t_ch: 佩佩！
t_en: Peppy!

t_ch: 佩佩？
t_en: Peppy?

t_ch: 嗯，它是我的爱犬，从小就由阿兰照看，他俩感情非常好。
t_en: Mm-hmm, Peppy's my dog — Arlan's looked after him since he was a puppy. They're great friends.

t_ch: 阿兰的气味，佩佩再熟悉不过了，或许它能找到阿兰。
t_en: Peppy knows Arlan's scent — maybe he can track him down.

t_ch: 那这里没我什么事呀？
t_en: Then... am I still needed?

t_ch: 不不，你想啊，我作为代理站长，要是当着这么多科员的面调查阿兰的行踪。
t_en: Y—Yes! Think about it — if people see me, the acting lead researcher, searching for Arlan...

t_ch: 那你快让它去找啊。
t_en: Then get Peppy to find Arlan.

t_ch: 让它自己去，要是也失踪了怎么办？可是我跟着它，当着这么多科员的面调查阿兰的行踪。
t_en: Peppy can't go alone. What if he gets lost too? And I can't go with him — if people see me, the acting lead researcher, searching for Arlan...

t_ch: 这岂不是…岂不是让他这个防卫科负责人显得很不靠谱！
t_en: Wouldn't that... Wouldn't that make Arlan look incompetent as head of the Security Department?

t_ch: 咱们这就带上佩佩出发吧！放心啦，它不会咬人的。
t_en: ...Let's go together! Relax — Peppy doesn't bite.

t_ch: 那是…卡波特教授？你确定阿兰来过这里？
t_en: That's... Professor Capote? Are you sure Arlan was here, Peppy?

t_ch: 汪！
t_en: Woof!

t_ch: 怪起来了，他找这中央空调能有什么事？
t_en: Weird. Why would he visit this Casanova?

t_ch: 中央空调？
t_en: Casanova?

t_ch: 看到那些个围在他身边的粉丝，你还不明白么？
t_en: Look at all his fans! Need I say more?

t_ch: 这就是空间站「这位科员真绝了」大赛第一名的含金量啊。
t_en: Behold, the winner of the "Most Awesome Researcher" contest.

t_ch: 这么说不好吧…
t_en: I thought you said his name was Capote...

t_ch: 啊哈哈，空间站公认的…不小心说顺口了。
t_en: Haha, he probably should've been named Casanova... I've gotten used to saying it, anyway.

t_ch: 这就是空间站「这位科员真绝了」大赛第一名的含金量啊。
t_en: Behold, the winner of the "Most Awesome Researcher" contest.

t_ch: 唔，我不方便亲自出面…{NICKNAME}，向卡波特问出情报的事儿就拜托你了。
t_en: Hmm, it's better if I don't show my face... {NICKNAME}, see if you can get some intel out of him.

t_ch: 其实我当时也很恐惧，也很害怕！但是我的心中仍有一束微光，引导着我向前迈步！
t_en: I must confess that I, too, was terrified. Petrified, even! But there remained a light in my heart. A light that led me onward!

t_ch: 那束光，正是大家幸福的笑容。
t_en: That light... was the happy smiles on all your faces!

t_ch: 啊——波少心里有我！
t_en: Ah! Master Capote thinks about me!

t_ch: （频频点头，表示认同）
t_en: (Nod in approval)

t_ch: 哦，是你啊。好久不见。最近过得还好吗？
t_en: Oh, it's you! Long time no see. How are you?

t_ch: 我现在有点忙，如果不是什么要紧事的话待会儿再说。
t_en: I'm a bit busy here. If it's not urgent, come back later.

t_ch: （目瞪口呆，嗤之以鼻）
t_en: (Look skeptical and scoff)

t_ch: 哦，是你啊。好久不见！最近过得不太好吗？怎么看你表情很复杂，眉毛都拧到一起去了？
t_en: Oh, it's you! Long time no see. Are you okay? You look troubled — that's quite a wrinkled brow.

t_ch: 来来来，和大家一起，听我讲讲之前空间站入侵的事情。回顾那段往事，可以有助于你走出迷茫。
t_en: Come! Join the others and listen to me recount the tale of the Legion's invasion. I'm sure your memory of the events will dispel your worries.

t_ch: 你听卡波特说了很久，逐渐忘记了过来问询的原本目的……
t_en: You've listened to Capote for a long time, thus gradually forgetting what you're going to ask...

t_ch: 当「反物质军团」与我擦肩而过的刹那，心里浮现的正是你们的容颜。坦白说没有你们，就没有现如今活生生的我。
t_en: The Antimatter Legion barely missed me, and that's when I saw your faces... It was all I could see. If it weren't for you, I wouldn't be standing here today.

t_ch: 啊——我会支持波少一辈子！
t_en: Ah! Long live Master Capote!

t_ch: （频频点头，表示认同）
t_en: (Nod in approval)

t_ch: 嗯嗯，看来你已经扫除了心中的迷茫。从你的眉眼之间，我就读出了四个大字：「豁然开朗」！
t_en: Aha, I see you no longer look troubled. There is only one word to describe your expression: Enlightenment!

t_ch: 怎么样？听我聊天，果然能感受到能量围绕在身旁吧？
t_en: Such is the power of my rhetoric! It creates an energy that uplifts the soul.

t_ch: （目瞪口呆，嗤之以鼻）
t_en: (Look skeptical and scoff)

t_ch: 诶，你怎么回事，是哪里不舒服吗？怎么脸色越来越差，瞳孔都要涣散了…没有关系！我卡波特的爱好就是帮助迷茫之人，接下来我会提升强度，为你提供心灵上的支持。
t_en: Oh, still troubled? You must be ill! You look pale and your pupils are gray... That's fine! I, Capote, never waver in my support! I shall double my efforts to heal your heart.

t_ch: 你向卡波特问询了关于阿兰的事情……
t_en: You've asked Capote about Arlan...

t_ch: 阿兰队长？这个人嘛…我当然是认识的，毕竟我的职责就是照亮空间站每一个人的心灵。但是至于他现在人在哪儿……
t_en: Arlan? Uh, yes... I know him. After all, my calling is to light up the heart of every person on the space station. As to his current location...

t_ch: 恕我不能回答！
t_en: Forgive me, but I cannot tell!

t_ch: （总感觉他那戏剧化的停顿和大喊都是精心设计的……）
t_en: (Insert dramatic pause and sudden drama here...)

t_ch: 大家都知道，我卡波特人生中最关键的词语，就是「原则」。如若没有了原则，人和机械臂又有什么区别？
t_en: Everyone knows the most important word in my life is "principle." Without principle, what difference would there be between a person and a mechanical arm?

t_ch: 可是上次波少说自己人生中最关键的词语是「光明」……
t_en: Wait a minute... last time he said the most important word in his life was "illumination..."

t_ch: 回去更新一下「波少个人百科」！这次是人生关键词<unbreak>12.0</unbreak>版本。
t_en: We need to update Master Capote's Life Wiki! This will be version <unbreak>12.0</unbreak> of his life keywords!

t_ch: 咳…嗯。总之，我是个不会说出谎言的人。我可以坦然告诉你，我与阿兰队长确实在最近有见过面，也知晓他的行踪。
t_en: Ahem, uh... Anyway, I do not lie. I can tell you that I did indeed meet Arlan recently, and I know where he went.

t_ch: 但是很抱歉。我已经用我的生命发起誓言：卡波特，绝不会将阿兰队长的秘密，告诉任何人！
t_en: Regrettably, I already swore on my life that I, Capote, would never tell anyone Captain Arlan's secret!

t_ch: （夸大其词强调情况的急迫性）
t_en: (Exaggerate the urgency and importance of the situation)

t_ch: 你还不懂，不懂我想要表达的含义！
t_en: You don't understand what I'm trying to say at all!

t_ch: 所谓危机，所谓灾难，不过都是现实物欲世界的虚影！然而人类的品格，人类的誓言与约定，才是最最珍贵，最最值得保留的东西！
t_en: Crises, disasters... these are just mirages of the material plane! A person's character and the vows of humankind are most precious — they must be protected at all costs!

t_ch: 你只看得见眼前那小小的风险，却不知道：我卡波特如果成了那样卑劣的小人，有多少少女将会从此感受不到快乐的滋味？
t_en: All you see are the minuscule risks of the immediate situation... but did you ever consider that if I, Capote, abandoned my principles, then the hopes and dreams of hundreds of fair ladies would be dashed?

t_ch: 我是她们的灯塔，所以我——「绝•不•蒙•尘」！
t_en: I am their lighthouse, and I will NEVER allow myself to be tainted!

t_ch: 呜呜呜，波少，波少真的太温柔了。
t_en: *sniff* Oh, Master Capote is so considerate...

t_ch: 你听卡波特说了很久，逐渐忘记了过来问询的原本目的……
t_en: You've listened to Capote for a long time, thus gradually forgetting what you're going to ask...

t_ch: （清醒一点！回忆起来我来这里的目的是什么！）
t_en: (Wow, the cringe is strong... I need to remember what I came here for!)

t_ch: （妙语连珠夸赞卡波特的品格）
t_en: (Spares no effort in praising Capote's character)

t_ch: 看来你已经非常了解我的为人了。
t_en: Seems like you already know what kind of person I am.

t_ch: 之前阿兰队长过来拜托我的时候，神色与你颇为相仿。他那个时候也非常焦虑，迫切地希望我给他一个答案。
t_en: Captain Arlan had a similar expression to yours when he found me. He was anxious and desired an answer.

t_ch: 然而答案又怎是那样简单的东西？更何况他想要达成那样从心而外的「修行」，更是难上加难……
t_en: But answers are not easy to give. Besides, he wanted to master a trial of the heart that would thoroughly change him, and that would be even more difficult...

t_ch: 你想都想不到那是多么困难的「修行」，我光是配合他模仿那种说话的方式，就累得够呛！还好他…啊！咳咳，不说了，不说了！
t_en: You can't even imagine the difficulty of the trial. I was tired merely from the act of imitating that way of talking with him! Thankfully he... Ah! Ahem, no more. No more!

t_ch: （他的说辞和阿兰的工作规划对得上，看来他果然知道关键信息。）
t_en: (His words match Arlan's work plan records. Seems like he does have key information.)

t_ch: 怎么样，想必你已经彻底理解了我的为人吧？我劝你，知难而退，别在我这里费心思费口舌了。如你所见，我也是很「忙」的。
t_en: Seems like you already know what kind of person I am. I'm telling you, quit while you can and don't waste your time and effort here. You can see that I, too, am quite busy.

t_ch: （夸大其词强调情况的急迫性）
t_en: (Exaggerate the urgency and importance of the situation)

t_ch: 哎，想不到你竟然是这么顽固的家伙。看来你心灵之灯已经蒙尘许久了啊！
t_en: *sigh* I didn't take you to be so stubborn. The light in your heart has been clouded for too long!

t_ch: 不过我卡波特不会放弃每一个人，哪怕我多费点口舌，也会把道理给你讲明白的！
t_en: But I, Capote, will never give up on anyone. Even if I have to talk through the day and into the night, I will make you understand!

t_ch: 你听卡波特说了很久，逐渐忘记了过来问询的原本目的……
t_en: You've listened to Capote for a long time, thus gradually forgetting what you're going to ask...

t_ch: （清醒一点！回忆起来我来这里的目的是什么！）
t_en: (Wow, the cringe is strong... I need to remember what I came here for!)

t_ch: （妙语连珠夸赞卡波特的品格）
t_en: (Spares no effort in praising Capote's character)

t_ch: 你想都想不到那是多么困难的「修行」，我光是配合他模仿那种说话的方式，就累得够呛！还好他…啊！咳咳，不说了，不说了！
t_en: You can't even imagine the difficulty of the trial. I was tired merely from the act of imitating that way of talking with him! Thankfully he... Ah! Ahem, no more. No more!

t_ch: （他的说辞和阿兰的工作规划对得上，看来他确实知道关键信息。）
t_en: (His words match Arlan's work plans. Seems like he does have key information.)

t_ch: 你询问卡波特关于修行和从心的事情……
t_en: You've asked Capote about the trial of the heart...

t_ch: 我，我刚才可没说这个吧？你是不是听错了？
t_en: I—I didn't say that! Did you mishear it?

t_ch: 咳咳…我刚才说的应该是：「人生就是一场修行，做人也要从心如一。」
t_en: Ahem... What I said was "Life is a trial, and you need to be true to yourself."

t_ch: 所以你说的那些和阿兰队长有关的东西，我是完全不知道的。哎，我这边应该得去开会了，要不我们下次再聊？
t_en: So, I know nothing about anything you said about Captain Arlan. Ahem, I need to head to a meeting now. Can we talk later?

t_ch: （可不能让他就这么溜了，如果他实在不肯说的话，那就——）
t_en: (I can't let him get away like this. If he doesn't want to tell me, then...)

t_ch: 以开除为由，恐吓卡波特。
t_en: Threaten Capote with dismissal from the space station.

t_ch: 你，你竟然说得出这种话！
t_en: Y—You dare to utter such a threat!

t_ch: 我告诉你，且不论你有没有权利把我开除。今天就算是艾丝妲站长…哦不，哪怕是黑塔女士亲自来命令我，我卡波特也不会屈服！
t_en: Listen here, leaving aside the fact that you don't have the right to dismiss me, I wouldn't yield even if Lead Researcher Asta — no, if Madam Herta herself commanded me!

t_ch: 别说是开除我。哪怕你今天用我的命来威胁我，我也会对你大声说「不」！
t_en: Why not threaten to KILL me while you're at it? I've said it once and I'll say it again — I will not yield!

t_ch: 波少，波少他真的是太好了。此生无悔粉波少——
t_en: *sigh* Master Capote is so courageous... We're so lucky to be his fans...

t_ch: 波少之前承诺阿兰队长的时候，说的话和刚才一模一样、分毫不差。
t_en: He said the exact same thing when he promised Captain Arlan.

t_ch: 对，尤其是那段「用命威胁，大声说不」，真的是太催泪了呜呜呜呜。
t_en: Yes, the part about not yielding... It's making me tear up! *sob*

t_ch: 今天回去我就把当时的<color=#dbc291ff>「录音稿」</color>打印下来，我们粉丝会必须全文朗读并背诵！
t_en: I'm gonna print out the <color=#dbc291ff>transcript of the conversation</color> as soon as I get back today! The fan club needs to recite and memorize it!

t_ch: （录音？阿兰委托卡波特的过程被录下来了…？）
t_en: (Transcript? They've recorded Arlan's conversation with Capote...?)

t_ch: （没想到关键线索会以这种方式出现……）
t_en: (I didn't think a clue like that would suddenly pop up...)

t_ch: 以晋升为由，引诱卡波特。
t_en: Tempt Capote with a promotion.

t_ch: 你，你别乱说啊！那可是有严格考核制度的。
t_en: D—Don't joke about that! Promotions have a strict assessment process!

t_ch: 虽然我的确是听说，空间站会特别褒奖品德优秀的科员…但那都是无稽之谈，即便是岁数最大的前辈，都没有亲眼见证过呢！
t_en: I did hear that the space station gives special benefits to morally upstanding researchers... but those are just rumors — even the oldest staff have never witnessed it!

t_ch: 你少瞧不起我们波少了！他不是你想的那种人！
t_en: Stop looking down on Master Capote! He's not the kind of person you think he is!

t_ch: 我们波少视职级为粪土，在「波少个人百科」<unbreak>4.0</unbreak>版本中就曾记录过这件事！
t_en: Master Capote's Life Wiki, version <unbreak>4.0</unbreak>: Master Capote doesn't care about rank. Everyone knows that!

t_ch: 今天你就算是要让波少当空间站的代理站长，他也不会告诉你「修行」的秘密！
t_en: Even if you promoted Master Capote to acting lead researcher, he wouldn't tell you about the trial!

t_ch: 没…没错！我是不会为了那种东西就失去宝贵的品格的！
t_en: ...I—Indeed! I won't forsake my precious morality for something so base!

t_ch: 你啊你，怎么会想出这样的坏主意，歪点子呢？你不是迷失，你这是彻底走上了歧途！
t_en: How could you think up something so terrible? You're not just lost, my friend — you've completely strayed from the narrow path!

t_ch: 我得对你进行深度的思想教育才行，最好是咱们之后有空坐下来慢慢细说……
t_en: I need to give you a thorough education on human ethics — we'll need to sit down and go over it slowly...

t_ch: 不用麻烦波少！这种不正之风，我们就能帮你肃清！
t_en: No need to bother Master Capote! We'll fix this injustice for you ourselves!

t_ch: 你被波少粉丝后援会团团包围，被迫接受了漫长的思想教育，逐渐忘记了过来问询的原本目的……
t_en: You've been surrounded by the Master Capote Fan Club and listened to a frustratingly long lecture, thus gradually forgetting what you're going to ask...

t_ch: （清醒一点！回忆起来我来这里的目的是什么！）
t_en: (Wow, the cringe is strong... I need to remember what I came here for!)

t_ch: 看样子，她们已经和你说了很多了。你应该也做出了深刻的内心反思吧。
t_en: They went through everything with you? I'm sure you reflected deeply on your actions.

t_ch: 你之前和我聊的那些事情，我就当没有听到过，你也最好多多反思。
t_en: I'll pretend I never heard the things you said. Think before you act in future, please.

t_ch: 当然，如果反思得不够彻底，我们也可以私下交流交流…总之，我要先去开会了！
t_en: Of course, if you haven't reflected enough, we can always talk more in private... Right now, I need to hurry to a meeting!

t_ch: （可不能让他就这么溜了，如果他实在不肯说的话，那就——）
t_en: (I can't let him get away like this. If he doesn't want to tell me, then...)

t_en: Tempt Capote with a promotion.

t_ch: 你可真是，毫不悔改，腐朽得相当彻底！
t_en: You really know nothing about repentance! You're totally corrupt!

t_ch: 这回我就亲自和你讲讲这背后的……
t_en: Now, let me tell you exactly what—

t_ch: 波少且慢！你没必要亲自教导，不然这家伙赚死了。
t_en: Wait, Master Capote! Don't impart your wisdom to this person. They don't deserve it!

t_ch: 就是就是，我们都没有和波少一对一交流的机会呢。
t_en: That's right! Even we don't get to receive personal instruction from Master Capote!

t_ch: 交给我们就好！波少退下吧！
t_en: Leave it to us, Master Capote!

t_en: You've been surrounded by the Master Capote Fan Club and listened to a frustratingly long lecture, thus gradually forgetting what you're going to ask...

t_ch: （清醒一点！回忆起来我来这里的目的是什么！）
t_en: (Wow, the cringe is strong... I need to remember what I came here for!)

t_ch: （刚才提到录音稿的，好像是这个人…）
t_en: (This is the one who mentioned the transcript just now...)

t_ch: 喂，你这家伙从刚才起就一直在波少旁边问来问去，难不成…也想加入后援会？
t_en: Hey, you've been talking Master Capote's ear off this whole time. Do you... want to join the fan club?

s_en: Ruminative Researcher after a Long Period of Thinking
t_ch: 确实有这个可能…不然怎么会知道阿兰队长找过波少的事情呢？
t_en: I bet you do... You wouldn't have known about Captain Arlan's meeting with Master Capote unless you had an interest.

t_ch: 想不到，你竟然连「波少个人百科」<unbreak>12.0</unbreak>版本的内部测试版内容，都已经了然于胸了！
t_en: You even memorized the internal testing content for version <unbreak>12.0</unbreak> of Master Capote's Life Wiki!

s_en: Ruminative Researcher after a Long Period of Thinking
t_ch: 看来这位确实是隐藏在人群中的波少资深粉丝，我们还是大意了。
t_en: You must be one of those super fans that stayed under the radar. *sigh* We've been careless.

t_ch: 既然是一家人，就不说两家话。来来来，这是我们最新版的波少粉丝后援会入会大礼包，再额外送你一份「波少个人百科」更新版内容！
t_en: We're on the same side after all! Here, this is our latest welcome bundle for new members of the fan club, and I'll toss in an updated version of Master Capote's Life Wiki!

t_ch: 这份更新内容里，可有卡波特大人难得一见的样子哦~
t_en: Aww... He looks really cool in the new limited-edition snapshots~

t_ch: 你将刚才发生的事情与收获的线索同步给了艾丝妲……
t_en: You've told Asta about what just happened and the clues you found...

t_ch: 哎，阿兰究竟有什么想不开的，需要油嘴滑舌的卡波特来协助他修行呢？
t_en: *sigh* What happened to you, Arlan? Why did you have to get that sleazy Capote to help you with a trial?

t_ch: 先看看这些物料吧。
t_en: Let's look at these materials first.

t_ch: 也只能从这些线索入手了。
t_en: Yeah, I guess that's our starting point.

t_ch: 那个卡波特确实有点东西…
t_en: That Capote does have something about him...

t_ch: 听说过，他好像很有号召力…哎哎，不谈题外话，先看看线索吧。
t_en: I heard he's good at rallying people to his cause... Ugh, never mind, let's stay on track and find some clues.

t_ch: 查看粉丝回馈手卡。
t_en: Read the fan reply card.

t_ch: 这个字迹有点眼熟…？像是阿兰的…
t_en: This handwriting looks familiar... Is it Arlan's...?

t_ch: 「——美丽娇艳的花儿也经不起烈日的灼晒，卡波特将永远在你身后给予你柔情与关怀。」
t_en: "Against the scorching sun, a beautiful flower will never endure. Within my fair embrace, my love for you is tender and pure."

t_ch: 这么油腻…感觉的确是卡波特的手笔。
t_en: Yuck, so slimy... it does sound like Capote.

t_ch: 查看限定写真。
t_en: Look at the limited-edition snapshot.

t_ch: 这张照片怎么看都是从角落里偷偷拍摄的…不过卡波特教授应该知情才对，毕竟上面都有他自己的亲笔签名。
t_en: Looks like a candid shot... but surely he knew it was being taken — it's autographed.

t_ch: 照片里好像还有半个身影，这个是…阿兰？！
t_en: There's a figure that's not quite in the shot. Is that... Arlan!?

t_ch: 说明阿兰的确来找过卡波特教授，证据确凿。
t_en: So Arlan did go to see Professor Capote. This is the evidence.

t_ch: 查看波少百科<unbreak>12.0</unbreak>。
t_en: Read Master Capote's Life Wiki version <unbreak>12.0</unbreak>.

t_ch: …空间站手册的更新都没这么认真频繁呢。
t_en: ...Even the space station handbook doesn't get updated this frequently.

t_ch: 这是…录音转文字的稿件？
t_en: Is this... an audio transcript?

t_ch: 你要进入状态，阿兰队长。
t_en: Keep your chin up, Captain Arlan.

t_ch: 抱歉教授。我实在没有办法把你想象成一个…强势的上司。
t_en: I'm sorry, professor. I just can't think of you as an... an overbearing boss.

t_ch: 你闭着眼来感受，毕竟你这个是从心的修行嘛！听我指挥，再来一次……
t_en: Close your eyes and feel the trail of the heart! Follow me, try once again...

t_ch: 咳咳嗯…阿兰！快来我办公室一趟！我的时间可是分分钟不等人，很昂贵的。
t_en: *cough* ...Arlan! Come to my office at once! My time is money and waits for no one.

t_ch: 是…是的！好，我现在就过来。
t_en: Y—Yes! I'm coming right now!

t_ch: 错！大错特错！阿兰队长，你不能这么逆来顺受呀。如果对方给你安排的是工作范围之外的内容，难道你也要立刻过去吗？
t_en: Wrong! Totally wrong! Captain Arlan, you can't listen to whatever other people said to you. What if they assign you some tasks outside your scope of work? Are you going to take them anyway?

t_ch: 哎！要想学会与强势的领导相处，你的从心修行还有很远的路要走啊！
t_en: You have many trials of the heart to take before you can handle an overbearing boss!

t_ch: 已经得出足够的线索了。
t_en: We have enough clues.

t_ch: …从心的修行，原来是指与「强势的上司」相处吗？
t_en: So... the trial of the heart means knowing how to deal with an overbearing boss.

t_ch: 阿兰平时…居然是这么看我的……
t_en: So that's... how Arlan sees me...

t_ch: 或许说的不是你呢？
t_en: Maybe it isn't about you.

t_ch: 你也不要太自责了。
t_en: Don't be too hard on yourself.

t_ch: 得和他好好谈谈，先找到阿兰吧。
t_en: I need to talk to him — let's hurry up and find him.

t_ch: 佩佩，又该你出场了。
t_en: Peppy, it's your turn again.

t_ch: 那是…丹恒和防卫科的人！
t_en: That's... Dan Heng and the Security Department staff!

t_ch: 「反物质军团」果然还有漏网之鱼藏匿在空间站内。走，我们也去搭把手！
t_en: There are still remnants of the Antimatter Legion in the space station. Let's go help them!

t_ch: …你们怎么来了？
t_en: ...What are you doing here?

t_ch: 我来守护朋友的秘密。
t_en: I'm here to protect a friend's secret.

t_ch: 我来寻找走丢的朋友。
t_en: I'm here to find a lost friend.

t_ch: …那你多加小心，此地还有些「军团」残党。
t_en: ...Then be careful. There are still Legion remnants in the area.

t_ch: 阿兰不在，空间站的安保需要格外谨慎，请诸位拿出十二分的决心与集中力。
t_en: Arlan isn't here right now, so we need to step up the space station's security. Spare no effort and stay focused.

t_ch: 是！代理大哥！
t_en: Yes, brother Dan Heng!

t_ch: 你见过阿兰？
t_en: You saw Arlan?

t_ch: 嗯，他在三月七那儿。
t_en: Yes, he's with March.

t_ch: 好一副领导的派头…
t_en: Check you out, calling the shots...

t_ch: 少开这种玩笑，我只是帮阿兰个忙。你是来找他的吧？他在三月七那儿。
t_en: *sigh* I'm only helping Arlan. You're here to look for him, right? He's with March.

t_ch: 但这是属于他一个人的修行，最好别去打扰。
t_en: But I suggest you don't bother him. He's given himself a personal trial.

s_en: Thrilled Member of the Security Department
t_ch: 大哥的特殊修行？不愧是男人中的男人，老虎中的雄狮！即便已经到了如此高度，都还要不断精进！
t_en: Brother Arlan's special trial? He's a man among men, a lion among tigers! He's already reached great heights, but still he wants to improve!

s_en: Excited Member of the Security Department
t_ch: 修行…听上去肯定比体能锻炼还要厉害！大哥每天都在挑战体能上限，一个人围着空间站跑<unbreak>21358</unbreak>米！孤狼中的雄鹰！永远高标准严要求！
t_en: That trial... I bet it's even more intense than physical training! And brother Arlan already challenges himself to the limit in that department — he runs <unbreak>21,358</unbreak> meters around the space station every day! An eagle among wolves! Always so hard on himself!

t_ch: 究竟是什么修行？
t_en: What kind of trial is it?

s_en: Thrilled Member of the Security Department
t_ch: 我知道！修行嘛，肯定是磨炼心性的。大哥他肯定是找到了一处瀑布，然后请求三月七小姐替他把水流的温度降至冰点！
t_en: I think it must have something to do with the mind. He probably found a waterfall and asked Ms. March to lower the temperature to freezing point!

s_en: Excited Member of the Security Department
t_ch: 你胡说！修行可不止是瀑布那么简单！依我看，大哥他肯定是想了解更多的战斗技巧，所以去麻烦见多识广的三月七小姐了。
t_en: No way! Brother Arlan's trial wouldn't be as simple as just standing under a waterfall! I think he wants to learn fighting skills from Ms. March.

t_ch: ……虽不中亦不远矣。
t_en: ...They're not correct, but not wrong either.

t_ch: 阿兰来找我，想学习如何消解上司带来的压力。
t_en: Arlan came to me to learn how to manage pressure from his boss.

t_ch: ——所以我让他去找三月七。如果连三月那家伙都遭得住，他也没什么可畏惧的了。
t_en: ...So I sent him to March. If he can manage her, then he has nothing to fear.

t_ch: 不愧是骏马中的巨熊！
t_en: He's a bear among horses!

s_en: Thrilled Member of the Security Department
t_ch: 总感觉这家伙的比喻怪怪的。
t_en: That might be a metaphor too far.

s_en: Excited Member of the Security Department
t_ch: 大哥他跑起来如骏马，战斗的时候如巨熊，我觉得很贴切！
t_en: Brother Arlan runs like a horse and fights like a bear. I think that's a great metaphor!

t_ch: 你小子的点子挺毒啊。
t_en: That's pretty sadistic, Dan Heng.

s_en: Thrilled Member of the Security Department
t_ch: 我倒觉得代理大哥挺机灵的。
t_en: I think brother Dan Heng is pretty smart.

s_en: Excited Member of the Security Department
t_ch: 大哥全身上下就剩下这么个小弱点，还如此追求上进！实在是太厉害了！
t_en: Brother Arlan only has that one weakness in his armor, but he won't ignore it! He's too awesome for that!

t_ch: 我和阿兰交换了任务，他和三月一起去调查空间站出现的可疑结晶体，我代替他来巡逻。
t_en: ...Arlan and I decided to switch our assignments. He went with March to investigate a suspicious crystal, and I've taken up his patrol.

t_ch: 就这样。
t_en: That's all.

t_ch: 阿兰有没有提起什么是从镜？
t_en: "Of the mirror..." Did Arlan mention what that meant?

t_ch: 他提到说自己在空间站阅读到一册没有署名的手写笔记，里面记录了化解上级压力的<unbreak>36</unbreak>种方法。
t_en: He said he read an anonymous handwritten journal in the space station — it contained <unbreak>36</unbreak> different strategies to do with managing pressure from one's boss.

t_ch: 还有一部分是有关修行的笔记。
t_en: It also contained some information on trials.

t_ch: 首先是从心——直面心中产生的这股压力，从而更好地面对它。
t_en: Firstly, the trial of the heart. One needs to acknowledge the pressure in one's heart, and only then can one face it.

t_ch: 然后是从镜——像是镜面反射一样，亲自体验别人在职场中所面临的压力，从而多元地感知它。
t_en: Secondly, trial of the mirror. Like a mirror's reflection, one needs to personally experience the pressure others feel in a job in order to understand it.

t_ch: 最后是从我——将自己置身于压力当中，完成自我的蜕变。
t_en: Lastly, trial of the self. One places oneself within that pressure to complete a transformation.

t_ch: 所以他来找我，应该是想完成从镜的部分。
t_en: I believe he came to me to complete the trial of the mirror.

t_ch: 阿兰有没有留下其他什么东西？
t_en: Did Arlan leave anything with you?

t_ch: 他走之前给了我一张通行证，方便我在空间站内巡逻。
t_en: He gave me a pass for access across the space station while on patrol.

s_en: Thrilled Member of the Security Department
t_ch: 我这里发现了一块破碎的天花板，这个战斗痕迹，肯定是大哥打下来的！
t_en: I found a broken ceiling tile. This mark... it must be from where Brother Arlan smashed it down!

s_en: Excited Member of the Security Department
t_ch: 去去去，你那个算什么线索？我这边这块地板才算好吧？你瞧瞧这个脚印——尺码符合，发力方式符合。这个才是大哥战斗的最佳证明！
t_en: Move aside. What kind of clue is that? This floor tile I found is way better! Look at the footprint — it's the right size, not to mention the angle of motion... This is proof of brother Arlan in combat!

t_ch: 你知不知道阿兰为什么要修行？
t_en: Do you know why Arlan is undertaking this trial?

t_ch: 不知道。他没说，我没问。
t_en: No. He didn't say, and I didn't ask.

s_en: Thrilled Member of the Security Department
t_ch: 大哥提升自己还需要什么理由？我看你是一点都不懂他喔！
t_en: Brother Arlan doesn't need a reason to improve himself! You don't understand him at all!

s_en: Excited Member of the Security Department
t_ch: 大哥是咱们防卫科出了名的人猛心细，如果你不理解他行为的目的，可能是你看问题的角度不够长远。
t_en: Brother Arlan is a fierce fighter with an eye for detail. If you don't understand his reason for doing things, maybe that's because you're too zoomed in.

s_en: Thrilled Member of the Security Department
t_ch: 大哥这叫未雨绸缪！不愧是咱空间站的冷酷翱鹰、防卫科的群狼之首。
t_en: He's just staying ahead of the game is all! He's the space station's coldhearted hawk, the Security Department's alpha wolf!

t_ch: 没有什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 要找阿兰的话，联系三月吧。
t_en: If you want to find Arlan, just contact March.

t_ch: 职场压力…如何与强势上司相处……
t_en: Job pressure... how to manage your boss...

t_ch: 阿兰…对不起，我都不知道自己给了你这么多压力……
t_en: Arlan... I'm sorry. I never knew I was giving you so much pressure...

t_ch: 别急，先看看线索。
t_en: Calm down. Let's look at the clues first.

t_ch: 也未必他就不喜欢…
t_en: Maybe he doesn't mind...

t_ch: 嗯…我要深刻感受一下阿兰心中的压力，到底是什么样的。
t_en: Mm... I need to feel the pressure he's feeling so I can understand what it's all about.

t_ch: 阿兰ID的通行证
t_en: Arlan's ID Pass

t_ch: 啊，这张通行证，上面还有一张佩佩的大头照片……
t_en: Ah, there's a photo of Peppy on the pass...

t_ch: 我以为阿兰和佩佩亲如兄弟，才一直让他和佩佩一起玩，难道这种超出工作范围的委托，给了他很大的压力吗！
t_en: I got Arlan to look after Peppy because I thought they got along... but was that giving him too much extra pressure?

t_ch: 你看，他甚至把佩佩的照片打印下来，随时随地给自己上紧发条！
t_en: Maybe he printed out Peppy's picture as a way of forcing himself to remember his responsibility...

t_ch: 破碎的天花板
t_en: Broken Ceiling Tile

t_ch: 你看，这个天花板都被打碎成什么样了。阿兰他心里肯定是憋了很多苦楚和委屈，但是又无处发泄，最后只能对着怪物奋力一击！
t_en: Look how smashed up this ceiling tile is... Arlan must have bottled up his frustration and had no way of releasing it — so he took it out on the monsters!

t_ch: 脚印
t_en: Footprint

t_ch: 这个尺码的确是阿兰的，这双鞋还是我给他买的。
t_en: It's Arlan's shoe size alright — and this actually came from a pair of shoes I bought for him.

t_ch: 说不定，穿着这双鞋，对他而言也是枷锁，也是痛苦呢？
t_en: Maybe even these shoes are painful shackles for him...

t_ch: 要不咱还是别看了。
t_en: Let's skip the clues for now.

t_ch: 有道理…果然还是要快些找到阿兰。我必须亲自给他好好道歉才行！
t_en: Yeah... We need to find Arlan quickly. I need to apologize to him in person!

t_ch: 佩佩，咱们走。
t_en: Peppy, let's get going.

t_ch: （只是给佩佩看了一下三月七的照片，它就能确认她的位置吗？）
t_en: (So Peppy can locate March just by looking at a photo of her...)

t_ch: （……它真的是靠气味辨别吗？）
t_en: (...Is he really using scent to track?)

t_ch: 哇，这么可爱的狗狗！…哇，你也来了。
t_en: *gasp* What a cute doggy! ...Oh, you're here too.

t_ch: 阿兰没有和你在一起吗？
t_en: Is Arlan not with you?

t_ch: 我们之前一起行动，不过现在分开啦。
t_en: He was, but not anymore.

t_ch: 毕竟他主动要求一个人去调查，我也不好拒绝嘛～
t_en: He wanted to go investigating on his own, so I let him~

t_ch: 狗狗比我醒目吗？
t_en: Nice of you to acknowledge the canine before me.

t_ch: 怎么会呢！只不过它新鲜，你熟悉嘛。
t_en: Hey! He's a new face, and you're not.

t_ch: 不过，这狗狗有点眼熟…啊，难道是阿兰的佩佩？真可惜，你的主人不在这儿，他一个人去调查啦～
t_en: Huh, this little guy looks familiar... Ah! Are you Arlan's Peppy? Awwww I'm sowwie, your master isn't here. He's gone off to investigate~

t_ch: 他有没有提到修行？
t_en: Did he mention anything about a trial?

t_ch: 他确实一路上都在念叨什么「丹恒先生，我敬你是条汉子……」
t_en: He kept harping on about Dan Heng being a "true warrior" or something...

t_ch: 还有「和三月七小姐一起执行任务，应该可以完成最终的修行。」
t_en: Oh, he did say that completing an assignment with me would help him "complete the final trial."

t_ch: 嘿嘿，似乎本姑娘教了他很多东西，我还挺厉害的嘛。
t_en: Hehe, it felt like he learned a lot from me. Then again, everyone has a lot to learn from me, hee-hee~

t_ch: 为什么你们分开了？
t_en: Why did you split up?

t_ch: 我们一起调查可疑的晶体。但是阿兰说什么「不能完成自我的蜕变，一切就毫无意义。」……
t_en: We were investigating suspicious crystals together, but he said it was all pointless if he couldn't "transform" himself...

t_ch: 我看他压力好像很大的样子，就给他出了个好主意～
t_en: I could tell he was giving himself a lot of pressure, so I gave him a suggestion~

t_ch: 我说你既然这么想证明自己，那就别嘴上嘟囔，要拿出行动！…其实我随口说说的，我也不知道他在做哪门子的修行。
t_en: I told him actions speak louder than words when you wanna prove yourself... I was just thinking out loud really, I had no idea what kind of trial he was doing.

t_ch: 不过他听我说完这句话后，说了句什么「丹恒先生所言极是」？
t_en: But when he heard that, he said "Mr. Dan Heng was right."

t_ch: …就一个人走掉啦。我还给他离去的背影拍了一张照片呢，说实话，挺硬汉的！
t_en: And then he just left. I took a photo of him — he was doing one of those tough guy walks!

t_ch: 你们去找阿兰的话，带上咱一块儿吧。他一个人我还挺不放心的呢。
t_en: Come to think of it, maybe I shouldn't have left him alone. Take me with you if you're going after him!

t_ch: 之前在相关文献中确实听说过，根据星核的内部波动，裂界也会随之诞生出现。
t_en: I read that a Stellaron's internal fluctuations will cause a Fragmentum to appear.

t_ch: 阿兰他……
t_en: Did Arlan...

t_ch: 哎！放心放心！这东西本姑娘见得多了！一个两个的，问题不大。
t_en: Hey, relax! I've seen plenty of Stellarons — it'll be fine.

t_ch: 这里的侵蚀程度远比我想象中要严重。辛克尔夫人也反映过部分区域的清理工作进度缓慢……
t_en: The extent of the corrosion here is a lot worse than I imagined. Mrs. Hinkel did say the clean-up had been slow in places...

t_ch: 裂界确实会对周围空间造成很多影响啦。空间站电子设备这么多，是不是经常发生短路？
t_en: The Fragmentum has a big influence on the space around it. All this electronic equipment on the station must short-circuit pretty regularly, huh?

t_ch: 报告的确提到过监控异常以及模块报错…看来之后可以从这方面入手解决问题。
t_en: The report did mention abnormalities in the surveillance system and module errors... We'll have to start prioritizing those issues.

t_ch: 裂界侵蚀的痕迹从这附近开始变得微弱了…也不知道它们是被什么力量控制影响的。
t_en: The Fragmentum corrosion is weaker here... I wonder what's controlling it?

t_ch: 怎么控制的我不知道，但是有一条规则是一定不会错的——
t_en: No clue, but I know one thing...

t_ch: 那就是裂界侵蚀的痕迹越弱，附近的情况就会越安全！
t_en: The less Fragmentum corrosion there is, the safer it gets!

t_ch: 希望阿兰那边平安无事吧。
t_en: I hope Arlan is safe.

t_ch: 小姐，你没事吧？
t_en: Are you okay, my lady?

t_ch: 阿兰…我……
t_en: Arlan... I...

t_ch: 小姐？你怎么哭了？是受伤了吗。
t_en: Lady Asta? Why are you crying? Are you hurt?

t_ch: 对不起！
t_en: I'm sorry!
Huh...?

t_ch: 都怪我平时给你这么大的压力，害得你这么难受。甚至还专门去调查了那些文献笔记，只为了学会如何缓解我带给你的负面压力……
t_en: It's all my fault — I've been putting too much on your plate. That's why you looked up ways to manage the pressure...

t_ch: 我真的没有想到…那些事情会给你带来…那么大的压力。
t_en: I—I honestly didn't realize that... these things would bring you s—so much... stress.

t_ch: 我想说…对…对不起呜呜呜。
t_en: I just want to say... I... I'm sorry! *sob*

t_ch: 小姐，我……
t_en: My lady, I...

t_ch: 你没有带给我任何的压力！
t_en: You didn't give me any pressure!

t_ch: 阿兰，你不用再继续隐瞒我了。你的这场修行，我已经和{NICKNAME}彻头彻尾调查清楚了！
t_en: Arlan, you don't need to hide this from me any longer. {NICKNAME} and I know all about the trials you're putting yourself through!

t_ch: 如果你还不愿意接受我的道歉，那我只能一点一点，深刻反省，向你展示我的决心。
t_en: If you can't forgive me right now, then... I—I'll reflect further and... find a way to prove that I'm willing to change.

t_ch: 可是小姐……
t_en: But, my lady...

t_ch: 首先，是你的从心修行！你为了能够深入了解上司的心理，专程求助了卡波特教授，请求他与你模拟对话。
t_en: Firstly, your trial of the heart! You went to Professor Capote and asked him to simulate conversations with you so you could better understand your boss.

t_ch: 证据就是……
t_en: And the evidence is...

t_ch: 卡波特手写版粉丝回馈手卡。
t_en: Capote's handwritten fan reply card.

t_ch: 证据就是这张粉丝回馈手卡！
t_en: This handwritten fan reply card!

t_ch: 上面的字迹果然就是你的吧，阿兰！
t_en: This is your handwriting, Arlan!

t_ch: 不必为自己辩解。虽然这张手卡上面的署名是卡波特教授，但是你的笔迹习惯是无法抹消的。尤其是这个弯钩，和你小时候的手写日记一模一样。
t_en: No need to explain! This is Professor Capote's autograph alright, but you can't hide your handwriting. The way you join your letters is no different to your childhood diary entries.
